questions.prompt: A build passes all system tests, but a warehouse user says the new packing screen forces three extra clicks for their normal workflow. What stage caught this, and what does it mean?
options: System testing caught a bug; the build is broken
UAT caught a fitness-for-purpose gap; it was built right but may not be the right thing for the user
Nothing was caught; extra clicks are not a valid finding
Regression testing caught it; an old feature was reintroduced
questions.explanation: System tests confirm the spec is met. UAT is where real users judge whether the software fits how they actually work, which is exactly what surfaced here.

questions.prompt: Which test summary line gives a stakeholder the most useful basis for a decision?
options: Testing complete, quality is good, recommend shipping
Checkout: 47/50 passed; 1 high-severity defect open (payment fails for saved cards); residual risk: returning customers may be unable to pay
We ran 50 tests and most of them passed
Pass rate 94%, no further detail needed
questions.explanation: A useful report is data-backed and states impact and residual risk plainly, so a stakeholder can weigh the actual consequence rather than trust a vague summary.

questions.prompt: The release date is tomorrow. There is one open high-severity defect that blocks checkout on mobile. What is the honest QA recommendation?
options: Sign off as Go; the date is fixed and most paths work
Refuse to release and block the date yourself
Report the defect and its impact, recommend No-go or Go-with-conditions (e.g., hotfix first), and let the business owner decide
Lower the defect to low severity so the numbers look acceptable
questions.explanation: QA supplies data and a clear recommendation with risk stated plainly; the business owner makes the call. You neither hide the defect nor unilaterally block.

questions.prompt: Why should a test summary keep the results section and the recommendation section separate?
options: It makes the report longer and more official-looking
So facts (pass rates, open defects) are not blurred with judgment (the go/no-go call), letting readers verify the data and weigh your conclusion independently
Because regulations require exactly two sections
So the recommendation can be changed without anyone noticing
questions.explanation: Separating verifiable data from your judgment keeps the report honest and lets a decision-maker trust the numbers while evaluating your recommendation on its own merits.
